ItacPersonItem.Notes: Canon of the cathedral in Padua, Bombardini joined the Ricovrati in 1683, he became secretary in 1688-89, and in 1692. In Padua he was professor of canon law from 1691, and subsequently of criminal law until 1721. In 1684 Bombardini deleivered a presentation in the Academy: `Chi apportasse maggior utile all`impero romano, o Antonio Pio con la clemenza, o Alessandro Severo con il rigore`. He contributed with a sonnet to Compositioni ... Ricovrati per la morte ... Helena Cornaro Piscopia (1684).
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
